title: Antidesma orthogyne
taxonomy: Antidesma orthogyne belongs to the family Phyllanthaceae. This plant species is a part of the genus Antidesma, which comprises several species of flowering plants. The classification of Antidesma orthogyne places it within the order Malpighiales. Furthermore, it is classified under the subclass Rosidae and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification reflects the plant's botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Antidesma orthogyne is known to have various medicinal properties and uses, although more research is needed to confirm its full potential. It has been traditionally used in some parts of the world to treat various health issues. In particular, Antidesma orthogyne has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of certain diseases. Some studies suggest that extracts from the plant may have potential in the treatment of conditions such as fever and wounds. However, further studies are required to fully understand its medicinal applications.

preparations: Antidesma orthogyne preparations have been utilized in traditional medicine, primarily in the Pacific Islands, for various purposes. intro: Antidesma orthogyne, also known as Pusri or Sembrani, is a plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae, native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Antidesma orthogyne include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Antidesma orthogyne is often prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to treat various ailments, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
